front: A Windows PC blue-screens (BSOD). What's the general troubleshooting approach?
back: Note the stop code, boot to safe mode, and check recently installed drivers/updates as the likely trigger — BSODs are usually driver or hardware related.

front: What does booting into Safe Mode help isolate?
back: Whether a third-party driver or startup app is causing instability, since Safe Mode loads only minimal drivers/services.

front: sfc /scannow vs DISM — when do you reach for DISM instead?
back: When sfc can't fix a corrupted file, because sfc relies on a healthy Windows image to pull replacement files from — DISM repairs that underlying image.

front: What's the difference between 'Reset this PC (keep my files)' and 'Reset this PC (remove everything)'?
back: Both reinstall Windows; 'keep my files' preserves personal data, 'remove everything' wipes user data too.

front: List the malware removal process steps in order.
back: 1) Investigate/verify symptoms, quarantine the infected system (disconnect from network). 2) Disable System Restore. 3) Remediate: update definitions, scan and remove. 4) Schedule scans and updates going forward. 5) Re-enable System Restore and create a new clean restore point. 6) Educate the end user.

front: Why disable System Restore during malware remediation?
back: So you don't accidentally restore infected files back later from a restore point created while the system was already compromised.

front: Why is 'restart the device' a legitimate first troubleshooting step, not just a joke?
back: It clears transient state and applies any pending updates — a surprising number of issues resolve simply from that.

front: A mobile app crashes on launch after a recent OS update. What are common causes to check?
back: Corrupted install (reinstall), missing dependency, or incompatibility with the new OS version.

front: A phone shows unexpected data usage and unrecognized account activity. What should you check first?
back: Installed app list for anything unrecognized, and account activity logs — possible sign of a malicious app or compromised credentials.
